author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XIV. 23, 24.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when He had sent the multitudes away, He went up into the mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, He was there alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves:for the wind was contrary.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what purpose doth He go up into the mountain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To teach us, that loneliness and retirement is good, when we are to pray to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this view, you see, He is continually withdrawing into the wilderness, and there often spends the whole night in prayer, teaching us earnestly to seek such quietness in our prayers, as the time and place may confer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the wilderness is the mother of quiet; it is a calm and a harbor, delivering us from all turmoils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He Himself then went up thither with this object, but the disciples are tossed with the waves again, and undergo a storm, equal even to the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whereas before they had Him in the ship when this befell them, now they were alone by themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus gently and by degrees He excites and urges them on for the better, even to the bearing all nobly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly we see, that when they were first near that danger, He was present, though asleep, so as readily to give them relief; but now leading them to a greater degree of endurance, He doth not even this, but departs, and in mid sea permits the storm to arise, so that they might not so much as look for a hope of preservation from any quarter; and He lets them be tempest-tost all the night, thoroughly to awaken, as I suppose, their hardened heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the nature of the fear, which the time concurs with the rough weather in producing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with the compunction, He cast them also into a greater longing for Himself, and a continual remembrance of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly, neither did He present Himself to them at once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “in the fourth watch,” so it is said, “of the night, He went unto them, walking upon the sea;”instructing them not hastily to seek for deliverance; from their pressing dangers, but to bear all occurrences manfully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At all events, when they looked to be delivered, then was their fear again heightened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “When the disciples,” it is said, “saw Him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit: and they cried out for fear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and He constantly doth so; when He is on the point of removing our terrors, He brings upon us other worse things, and more alarming: which we see took place then also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For together with the storm, the sight too troubled them, no less than the storm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore neither did He remove the darkness, nor straightway make Himself manifest, training them, as I said, by the continuance of these fears, and instructing them to be ready to endure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This He did in the case of Job also; for when He was on the point of removing the terror and the temptation, then He suffered the end to grow more grievous; I mean not for his children’s death, or the words of his wife, but because of the reproaches, both of his servants and of his friends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when He was about to rescue Jacob from his affliction in the strange land, He allowed his trouble to be awakened and aggravated: in that his father-in-law first overtook him and threatened death, and then his brother coming immediately after, suspended over him the extremest danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since one cannot be tempted both for a long time and severely; when the righteous are on the point of coming to an end of their conflicts, He, willing them to gain the more, enhances their struggles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which He did in the case of Abraham too, appointing for his last conflict that about his child.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus even things intolerable will be tolerable, when they are so brought upon us, as to have their removal near, at the very doors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So did Christ at that time also, and did not discover Himself before they cried out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more intense their alarm, the more did they welcome His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Afterward when they had exclaimed, it is said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer, it is I; be not afraid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This word removed their fear, and caused them to take confidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as they knew Him not by sight, because of His marvellous kind of motion, and because of the time, He makes Himself manifest by His voice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Peter, everywhere ardent, and ever starting forward before the rest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Lord, if it be Thou,” saith he, “bid me come unto Thee on the water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “Pray and entreat,” but, “bid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how great his ardor, how great his faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely he is hereby often in danger, by seeking things beyond his measure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so here too he required an exceedingly great thing, for love only, not for display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did he say, “Bid me walk on the water,” but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Bid me come unto Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For none so loved Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This he did also after the resurrection; he endured not to come with the others, but leapt forward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not love only, but faith also doth he display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he not only believed that He was able Himself to walk on the sea, but that He could lead upon it others also; and he longs to be quickly near Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And he said, Come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, and cameto Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when he saw the wind boisterous,he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And immediately Jesus stretched forth His hand and caught him, and saith unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is more wonderful than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore this is done after that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He had shown that He rules the sea, then He carries on the sign to what is yet more marvellous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He rebuked the winds only; but now He both walks Himself, and permits another to do so; which thing if He had required to be done at the beginning, Peter would not have so well received it, because he had not yet acquired so great faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then did Christ permit him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if He had said, “thou canst not,” Peter being ardent would have contradicted Him again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore by the facts He convinces him, that for the future he may be sobered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so doth he endure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore having come down, he becomes dizzy; for he was afraid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this the surf caused, but his fear was wrought by the wind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But John saith, that “they willingly received Him into the ship; and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went,”relating this same circumstance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that when they were on the point of arriving at the land, He entered the ship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Peter then having come down from the ship went unto Him, not rejoicing so much in walking on the water, as in coming unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when he had prevailed over the greater, he was on the point of suffering evil from the less, from the violence of the wind, I mean, not of the sea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a thing is human nature; not seldom effecting great things, it exposes itself in the less; as Elias felt toward Jezebel, as Moses toward the Egyptian, as David toward Bathsheba.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so then this man also; while their fear was yet at the height, he took courage to walk upon the water, but against the assault of the wind he was no longer able to stand; and this, being near Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So absolutely nothing doth it avail to be near Christ, not being near Him by faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this also showed the difference between the Master and the disciple, and allayed the feelings of the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if in the case of the two brethren they had indignation, much more here; for they had not yet the Spirit vouchsafed unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But afterwards they were not like this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On every occasion, for example, they give up the first honors to Peter, and put him forward in their addresses to the people, although of a rougher vein than any of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did He not command the winds to cease, but Himself stretched forth His hand and took hold of him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because in him faith was required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when our part is wanting, then God’s part also is at a stand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Signifying therefore that not the assault of the wind, but his want of faith had wrought his overthrow, He saith, “Wherefore didst thou doubt, O thou of little faith?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if his faith had not been weak, he would have stood easily against the wind also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this reason, you see, even when He had caught hold of Him, He suffers the wind to blow, showing that no hurt comes thereby, when faith is steadfast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as when a nestling has come out of the nest before the time, and is on the point of falling, its mother bears it on her wings, and brings it back to the nest; even so did Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when they were come into the ship, then the wind ceased.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas before this they had said, “What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey Him!”now it is not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “they that were in the ship,” it is said, “came and worshipped Him, saying, Of a truth Thou art Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how by degrees he was leading them all higher and higher?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both by His walking on the sea, and by His commanding another to do so, and preserving him in jeopardy; their faith was henceforth great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then indeed He rebuked the sea, but now He rebukes it not, in another way signifying His power more abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they said, “Of a truth Thou art Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did He rebuke them on their so speaking?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, quite the contrary, He rather confirmed what they said, with greater authority healing such as approached Him, and not as before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when they were gone over,” so it is said, “they came into the land of Gennesaret.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when the men of that place had knowledge of Him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto Him all that were diseased; and besought Him that they might touch the hem of His garment; and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did they approach Him as before, dragging Him into their houses, and seeking a touch of His hand, and directions from Him in words; but in a far higher strain, and with more of self-denial, and with a more abundant faith did they try to win themselves a cure; for she that had the issue of blood taught them all to be severe in seeking wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the evangelist, implying also that at long intervals He visited the several neighborhoods, saith, “The men of that place took knowledge of Him, and sent out into the country round about, and brought unto Him them that were diseased.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet the interval, so far from abolishing their faith, made it even greater, and preserved it in vigor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us also then touch the hem of His garment, or rather, if we be willing, we have Him entire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed His body is set before us now, not His garment only, but even His body; not for us to touch it only, but also to eat, and be filled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us now then draw near with faith, every one that hath an infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they that touched the hem of His garment drew from Him so much virtue, how much more they that possess Him entire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now to draw near with faith is not only to receive the offering, but also with a pure heart to touch it; to be so minded, as approaching Christ Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what, if thou hear no voice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet thou seest Him laid out; or rather thou dost also hear His voice, while He is speaking by the evangelists.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Believe, therefore, that even now it is that supper, at which He Himself sat down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is in no respect different from that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither doth man make this and Himself the other; but both this and that is His own work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore thou seest the priest delivering it unto thee, account not that it is the priest that doeth so, but that it is Christ’s hand that is stretched out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even as when he baptizes, not he doth baptize thee, but it is God that possesses thy head with invisible power, and neither angel nor archangel nor any other dare draw nigh and touch thee; even so now also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when God begets, the gift is His only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not those who adopt to themselves sons here, how they commit not the act to slaves, but are themselves present at the judgment-seat?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so neither hath God committed His gift to angels, but Himself is present, commanding and saying, “Call no man Father on earth;”not that thou shouldest dishonor them that gave thee birth, but that thou shouldest prefer to all those Him that made thee, and enrolled thee amongst His own children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He that hath given the greater, that is, hath set Himself before thee, much more will He not think scorn to distribute unto thee of His body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us hear therefore, both priests and subjects, what we have had vouchsafed to us; let us hear and tremble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of His own holy flesh He hath granted us our fill; He hath set before us Himself sacrificed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What excuse shall we have then, when feeding on such food, we commit such sins? when eating a lamb, we become wolves? when feeding on a sheep, we spoil by violence like the lions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this mystery He directs to be always clear, not from violence only, but even from bare enmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for this mystery is a mystery of peace; it allows us not to cling to wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He spared not Himself for us, what must we deserve, sparing our wealth, and being lavish of a soul, in behalf of which He spared not Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now upon the Jews God every year bound in their feasts a memorial of His peculiar favors to them: but for thee, every day, as I may say, through these mysteries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not therefore ashamed of the cross: for these are our venerable things, these our mysteries; with this gift do we adorn ourselves, with this we are beautified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if I say, He stretched out the heaven, He spread out the earth and the sea, He sent prophets and angels, I say nothing in comparison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the sum of His benefits is this, that “He spared not His own Son,”in order to save His alienated servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no Judas then approach this table, no Simon; nay, for both these perished through covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us flee then from this gulf; neither let us account it enough for our salvation, if after we have stripped widows and orphans, we offer for this table a gold and jewelled cup.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, if thou desire to honor the sacrifice, offer thy soul, for which also it was slain; cause that to become golden; but if that remain worse than lead or potter’s clay, while the vessel is of gold, what is the profit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let not this therefore be our aim, to offer golden vessels only, but to do so from honest earnings likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these are of the sort that is more precious even than gold, these that are without injuriousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the church is not a gold foundry nor a workshop for silver, but an assembly of angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it is souls which we require, since in fact God accepts these for the souls’ sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That table at that time was not of silver nor that cup of gold, out of which Christ gave His disciples His own blood; but precious was everything there, and awful, for that they were full of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wouldest thou do honor to Christ’s body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neglect Him not when naked; do not while here thou honorest Him with silken garments, neglect Him perishing without of cold and nakedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He that said, “This is my body,” and by His word confirmed the fact, “This same said, “Ye saw me an hungered, and fed me not;” and, “Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For This indeed needs not coverings, but a pure soul; but that requires much attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us learn therefore to be strict in life, and to honor Christ as He Himself desires.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to Him who is honored that honor is most pleasing, which it is His own will to have, not that which we account best.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since Peter too thought to honor Him by forbidding Him to wash his feet, but his doing so was not an honor, but the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so do thou honor Him with this honor, which He ordained, spending thy wealth on poor people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since God hath no need at all of golden vessels, but of golden souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things I say, not forbidding such offerings to be provided; but requiring you, together with them, and before them, to give alms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He accepts indeed the former, but much more the latter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the one the offerer alone is profited, but in the other the receiver also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here the act seems to be a ground even of ostentation; but there all is mercifulness, and love to man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is the profit, when His table indeed is full of golden cups, but He perishes with hunger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First fill Him, being an hungered, and then abundantly deck out His table also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou make Him a cup of gold, while thou givest Him not a cup of cold water?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is the profit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou furnish His table with cloths bespangled with gold, while to Himself thou affordest not even the necessary covering?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what good comes of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, should you see one at a loss for necessary food, and omit appeasing his hunger, while you first overlaid his table with silver; would he indeed thank thee, and not rather be indignant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, again, if seeing one wrapped in rags, and stiff with cold, thou shouldest neglect giving him a garment, and build golden columns, saying, “thou wert doing it to his honor,” would he not say that thou wert mocking, and account it an insult, and that the most extreme?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let this then be thy thought with regard to Christ also, when He is going about a wanderer, and a stranger, needing a roof to cover Him; and thou, neglecting to receive Him, deckest out a pavement, and walls, and capitals of columns, and hangest up silver chains by means of lamps,but Himself bound in prison thou wilt not even look upon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things I say, not forbidding munificence in these matters, but admonishing you to do those other works together with these, or rather even before these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because for not having done these no one was ever blamed, but for those, hell is threatened, and unquenchable fire, and the punishment with evil spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not therefore while adorning His house overlook thy brother in distress, for he is more properly a temple than the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas these thy stores will be subject to alienations both by unbelieving kings, and tyrants, and robbers; whatever thou mayest do for thy brother, being hungry, and a stranger, and naked, not even the devil will be able to despoil, but it will be laid up in an inviolable treasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then doth He Himself say, “The poor always ye have with you, but me ye have not always?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, for this reason most of all should we give alms, that we have Him not always an hungered, but in the present life only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou art desirous to learn also the whole meaning of the saying, understand that this was said not with a view to His disciples, although it seem so, but to the woman’s weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, her disposition being still rather imperfect, and they doubting about her; to revive her He said these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that for her comfort He said it, He added, “Why trouble ye the woman?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And with regard to our having Him really always with us, He saith, “Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From all which it is evident, that for no other object was this said, but that the rebuke of the disciples might not wither the faith of the woman, just then budding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then bring forward these things now, which were uttered because of some economy, but let us read all the laws, those in the New and those in the Old Testament, that are set down about almsgiving, and let us be very earnest about this matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cleanses from sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “give alms, and all things will be clean unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is a greater thing than sacrifice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I will have mercy, and not sacrifice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This opens the heavens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is more indispensable than virginity: for thus were those virgins cast out of the bridechamber; thus were the others brought in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All which things let us consider, and sow liberally, that we may reap in more ample abundance, and attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever.
